summary_text:
Chief Complaint
Left knee pain

History of Present Illness
The patient reports intermittent left knee pain, occurring less than once a day when walking, often accompanied by a feeling of giving out. He describes holding grocery carts to provide support and stability, which helps alleviate the pain. The pain is fleeting, resolving quickly. He denies any history of trauma or injury. He has not taken any medication for the pain. The patient is planning a trip to Georgia in the next month, where he anticipates participating in line dances, but is hesitant to do so due to the knee pain. He reports no other symptoms or concerns.

Findings
Examination reveals no visible edema or erythema. Range of motion is reduced, with a slight apprehension in the left knee. Palpation elicits tenderness. Neurological exam is intact. Vital signs are stable: temperature 98.2°F, pulse 72, respirations 16, blood pressure 120/78. The patient's blood pressures have been running around that range for several days. He is currently taking farxiga, amlodipine, lisinopril, and hydrochlorothiazide, which he takes regularly in the morning and evening. His blood sugars have been running a little higher at about 130 in the morning. He has been told that the morning blood sugars are higher for some people. The patient's lipid panel will be ordered. A slight systolic ejection murmur is heard at the left base.

Assessment
The patient's symptoms and physical exam findings are most consistent with osteoarthritis of the left knee.

Plan
reference_summary:
CHIEF COMPLAINT

Left knee pain.

HISTORY OF PRESENT ILLNESS

Andrea Barnes is a 34-year-old female who presents today for evaluation of left knee pain.

The patient has been experiencing intermittent episodes of pain and sudden instability with ambulation. Her pain is localized deep in her patella and occurs less than once daily. Due to the fleeting nature of these episodes, she has not taken medication and simply braces herself until it passes. She denies any trauma or injury, or ever hearing or feeling a pop in the knee. Her symptoms do not interfere with her daily activities and she does not use a cane.

Regarding her hypertension, it has been several days since she last checked her blood pressure at home, but it was approximately 120/70 mmHg at that time. Her current medications include amlodipine, lisinopril, and hydrochlorothiazide, all of which she takes on a regular basis.

In terms of her diabetes, her fasting morning blood glucose levels have been approximately 130 based on home monitoring. This is slightly higher than usual, even though she has been compliant with metformin and Farxiga. She does try to avoid eating late at night.

MEDICAL HISTORY

Patient reports a personal history of hypertension and type 2 diabetes.

SOCIAL HISTORY

Patient likes to travel and is planning a trip to Columbus, Georgia in the next month or so. She is part of the Lion's Club.

MEDICATIONS

Patient reports that she is taking amlodipine, lisinopril 20 mg once daily, hydrochlorothiazide, Metformin, and Farxiga.

REVIEW OF SYSTEMS

Musculoskeletal: Reports left knee pain and instability,

VITALS

Temperature: 98.2 degrees F
Heart rate: 72 bpm
Respirations: 16
Blood pressure: 122/70 mmHg

PHYSICAL EXAM

MSK: Examination of the right knee: No ecchymosis or edema. No effusion. No pain with palpation.
Examination of the left knee: Full range of motion. Negative varus and valgus stress test.

RESULTS

X-rays were obtained and reviewed today. These reveal no evidence of fracture or bony abnormality.

ASSESSMENT

1. Left knee pain.
2. Hypertension.
3. Diabetes mellitus type 2.

PLAN

After reviewing the patients x-rays, I believe there is some arthritis in the knee. I'm going to prescribe meloxicam 15 mg once daily. We can consider physical therapy to strengthen the muscles around the area to prevent any further issues.

Her hypertension is well controlled with her current medication regimen. She can continue with lisinopril 20 mg once daily. I'm also going to order an echocardiogram for further evaluation of the murmur heard on exam.

She has been compliant with metformin as prescribed, but her blood glucose levels have been slightly elevated recently. I'm going to order a lipid panel, as well as a hemoglobin A1c to determine if any adjustments need to be made to her dose of metformin.

section_miscategorizations.text: He is currently taking farxiga, amlodipine, lisinopril, and hydrochlorothiazide, which he takes regularly in the morning and evening.
section_miscategorizations.reason: Current outpatient medication regimen and adherence pattern are part of the patient's history/medication state rather than an exam or test finding.

section_miscategorizations.text: His blood sugars have been running a little higher at about 130 in the morning.
section_miscategorizations.reason: Self-monitored home blood glucose trends are patient-reported history, not in-clinic objective findings.

section_miscategorizations.text: He has been told that the morning blood sugars are higher for some people.
section_miscategorizations.reason: This is patient-reported prior counseling/understanding, which belongs in the history rather than the objective findings section.

section_miscategorizations.text: The patient's lipid panel will be ordered.
section_miscategorizations.reason: This is a future diagnostic order and should be documented in the plan, not in the objective findings section.
